---
title: "Wzorce w przestrzeni"
subtitle: "*gdzie geografia spotyka matematykę*"
author: "Jakub Nowosad, <https://jakubnowosad.com/>"
institute: "Horyzonty Matematyki, Lublin"
date: "2026-03-26"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: figs/cover.png
  data-background-size: cover
  data-background-opacity: "1"
format:
  n3-revealjs:
    footer: "[jakubnowosad.com/horyzontymatematyki2026](https://jakubnowosad.com/horyzontymatematyki2026)"
    # slide-number: false
    progress: false
    margin: 0.05
    menu: false
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-fold: true
filters:
  - ripper
knitr: 
  opts_chunk: 
    out-width: 100%
    fig-align: center
    fig-height: 9
# https://artvee.com/dl/river-landscape-40#00    
# 45 minutes (plus also possible time for questions)    
---

## Summary

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%" .incremental}

- **Understand sampling design**, it affects model training & evaluation  
- **Align evaluation with prediction context**  
- **Use prediction-domain adaptive cross-validation** (e.g., kNNDM) for realistic assessment  
- **Check prediction reliability**, for example, using Area of Applicability (AoA)  
- **Leverage R tools** for spatial machine learning tasks
<!-- - Combining domain knowledge with appropriate methods leads to more reliable spatial predictions -->

:::
::: {.column width="45%" .faded-right}
<div style="text-align: right">
### Contact

Website: <https://jakubnowosad.com>

### Resources

Slides: <https://jakubnowosad.com/rome2025>

### Acknowledgments

*Various R developers*

*Contributors to the geocompx project*

*Members of the Remote Sensing and Spatial Modelling Research Group (especially Hanna Meyer and Jan Linnenbrink)*
<!-- - Participants of the "Advances in Spatial Machine Learning 2025" workshop -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| out-width: 75%
#| fig-align: right
knitr::include_graphics("figs/EN-Funded by the EU-POS.jpg")
```
</div>
:::
::::
